title: Perfect for a weekend stay in Wrocław
pos_text: Very clean rooms. An incredibly comfortable mattress to sleep on. Blackout curtains - they do not let any light through, so if you are a light sleeper, you don't have to worry. Fresh, fragrant bedding. Clean and spacious (although not large) bathroom, fully equipped - towels, cosmetics, washing gel, shampoo and a set for women (this is a huge plus!). Coffee, tea and chocolate available 24/7 downstairs in the lobby. The decor of the entire hotel is well thought out, very aesthetic, and pleasing to the eye. The location is a huge plus, right in the center of the city.
neg_text: If someone is a sensitive sleeper, it is worth asking for a room at the end of the corridor, with a window overlooking the courtyard. It was our first time at Puro and we got a room with a window facing the street, and since our stay was on a weekend, we could hear all the parties, garbage trucks, people returning from parties in the morning, and people talking in the hotel corridor at night. The price of the hotel parking lot does not encourage its use. A few steps from the hotel there is the NFM parking lot, underground, but cheaper. The prices at the bar downstairs by the hotel reception are high, not matching the room prices, and they also add a commission for up to two drinks. There was no information about this on the menu or before entering the card into the terminal. I realized it after the load. There is no fridge in the lowest standard rooms. For a short, weekend stay this place is absolutely fine.

title: Exceptional
pos_text:
One of the best hotels I've ever stayed in.
Amazing location, in the city center, next to the river, yet a very quiet street, not noisy whatsoever! Next to lots of public transport stops and the bus from the airport.

Very clean, very modern, comfortable rooms with everything you might need. The beds are super comfortable.

The room is "smart", you can control the TV, lights, temple, set an alarm, contact reception and many more things via it. You can even screencast your phone to the TV.

The breafast is great, doesn't vary day to day but there is enough variety to not get bored and everything was delicious.

Free coffee, tee, hot chocolate and apples, all dag and night, which is a great bonus.

Lots of useful services at the hotel, they can even get you a PlayStation if you request it (didn't try that but it was in the hotel info).

Didn't try the restaurant but it was priced very decently.

Very kind and helpful staff.

Overall, just an amazing experience and I'd love to come back!
neg_text:
The only thing is that I am not a fan of the "open" bathrooms with glass but they offer a curtain to cover this part of the room so you can have some privacy.

Also a second mirror besides the one in the bathroom would be great.

title: Excellent hotel in the center of Wroclaw
pos_text:
Everything was great! The hotel is clean. The room was very quiet (I wrote to the hotel in advance to ask for a room with windows facing the courtyard). They checked us in quickly and very politely. The room was newly renovated, everything was very clean and tidy. The bed linen and towels are snow-white. The mattresses are high and very comfortable. Mon was of good quality.

A hair dryer with a narrow nozzle is just what you need for styling your hair. TV with Netflix and Youtube.
The curtains are absolutely super - they darken the room, so you can get a great night's sleep.
Breakfasts are fresh. There was enough of everything, even if you came towards the end of breakfast. The coffee is excellent, the juices are not powdered, they are normal. All the food was fresh.

There were a lot of guests at the hotel, mostly groups from European countries, but in the corridors and rooms there was no noise or conversations from neighbors at all.

The location of the hotel is very convenient. In the center. The Market Square is a 10-15 minute walk away.

I recommend the hotel. We liked everything!
Thanks to the team of the Campanille Prime Hotel - Wroclaw Stare Miasto. When we come to Wroclaw, we will choose this hotel again!

The room is tiny and has "tenants" in the form of ants crawling around the bathroom and the room. After reporting the issue, the room was not changed, but "cleaned". On the same day in the evening, a similar situation occurred, i.e. the "tenants" returned. We were promised a room change the next day when another room became available. The next day in the afternoon we were given a room change but this time on the noisy side facing the main street towards the center, the windows seemed to be missing, the noise and traffic seemed to be constantly open. Sleeping pillows are like flat "pancakes", so sleeping problems and neck pain are guaranteed. The breakfast was very poor and meager, and the cleanliness was questionable. The bathroom had a bathtub the size of a baby's bathtub, and there was no socket in the bathroom, so you couldn't plug in your curling iron or hairdryer. The TV cannot connect to the internet, so if you have streaming services, forget about using them.
The hotel parking lot is a joke, tight, small, and the worst part is that if you buy a space and go out into the city, you can't be sure there will be a space for you when you return. Parking is prohibited on the streets around and the city guard is often present. THE HOTEL IS A FAILURE, WE WILL DEFINITELY NEVER GO BACK THERE.

- no information that the hotel is under renovation. As a guest, I came to the hotel to rest after my trip and the whole time I was listening to drilling in the walls literally next door. Additionally, as someone who struggles with migraines, it didn't help me. If there had been information about this, I would not have chosen this accommodation.
- breakfast was okay, but it's a shame that several ladies were chatting away at the reception desk, and there was no one to bring glasses or cutlery, or even clear the tables, lemon in "lemon water" also missing apparently. There was no milk for the cereal, nor for the coffee machine. You had to go around looking for the lady and ask her to do what she was paid for. Staff to be trained. We had to sit at a table that, although there were no plates left after the guests, was covered in crumbs and splashed with drink because it was the only table not as cluttered as the rest.
- very low quality meat, I'm not surprised there's so much of it left. The chairs in the dining room are all scratched up, the fabric is peeling, their glory days are behind them.

We had a fantastic stay at this hotel and can highly recommend it. Here are the points that particularly convinced us:

Prime location:
The hotel is located quite close to the center. We were able to reach almost everything comfortably on foot, which made exploring the city super relaxed. Despite the central location, the room was pleasantly quiet.

Plenty of space to feel comfortable:
We were particularly impressed by the large rooms. There's plenty of space for luggage and you don't feel cramped at all – something you rarely find in city hotels.

Cleanliness & Comfort:
Everything was spotless and the beds were very comfortable.

Service:
The staff were friendly and helpful, and check-in went smoothly.

The location was perfect, I was able to explore Wroclaw on foot without any issues.

The breakfast was great, safer options like cereal were available but also more cultural options like herring and kielbasa.

title: Very good
pos_text:
The hotel's location is unbeatable, very close to the market square. Free parking on the street from 8 pm to 9 am. Otherwise, hotel parking is available for approximately €12 per 24 hours.

The room was spacious, the bed comfortable but unfortunately equipped with cheap (uncomfortable) pillows. Room cleaning is available for an extra charge. Extensive breakfast buffet for approximately 15 euros per person. The refrigerator wasn't cooling properly. We booked the room cheaply on the day of arrival, so the price-performance ratio was right.
neg_text: The cheap pillow was uncomfortable.

title: Bad
neg_text: The hotel is reasonably located, parking is a problem. The hotel parking lot is quite small and it can be difficult to find a space, and finding a space in the area is very difficult. The biggest problem, however, is the service. I've stayed at this hotel several times and always had to wait a long time to check in - systems not working, mistakes, breakfast not included, parking charged separately, no option to add parking to the invoice, no invoice on site, asking for an invoice for weeks later (now it's OK because of KSEF), insufficient knowledge of the Polish language of the service,... Overall, the approach to the customer is unacceptable. After my last stay and a double payment from booking and my card, which ended with several emails and refusals to settle the matter by the hotel, I finally used the chargeback procedure and resolved the matter to my benefit. As I am often in Wrocław on business trips, I will no longer stay overnight with them.,

title: Second pleasant stay within the past year
pos_text: My second stay here within the past year. I slightly preferred the previous room but for the price there really is nothing to complain. The reception staff welcoming, the cleanliness excellent, the shower very good, the room perfectly quiet.

title: Does not match the photos or description
neg_text:
The photos in the listing are completely different from reality. The room does not match what is advertised at all. The hotel claims to offer a king-size bed, but the bed is smaller and uncomfortable. When we asked at the front desk, we were told that the hotel does not even have the advertised bed size.

There were plumbing renovations taking place, which caused a sewage smell in our room (ground floor). There is also no soundproofing — we could clearly hear loud music from the room next door.

We felt misled and decided to change hotels. For the same price, we stayed at a higher-category hotel in a better location in the historic city center. Honestly, it wouldn’t be worth it even at half the price we paid. We do not recommend it.

pos_text: Location, good breakfast
neg_text: Of the several hotels in Wrocław on the selected date, Dikul was chosen. Of course, I was guided by the photos of the rooms and I didn't even notice that there were no photos of the bathroom anywhere. Location, cleanliness in the room, breakfast ok. Unfortunately, after entering the room, consternation and surprise because the factual situation and photos are two different stories. The room was dark, as if it had come straight from the communist era, and the bathroom looked like a hospital bathroom from the communist era, the only thing missing was a white stool on metal legs. The nice lady at the reception told me that unfortunately all the rooms look like this, including the apartment offered. Yes, I think the photos were generated by AI or the owner's wishful thinking. I know there is competition on the market, etc., but I believe that honesty towards customers comes first. I suggest posting real photos of the rooms and bathrooms so that all customers can make an informed decision and not be unpleasantly surprised on the spot.

title: Is money everything?
pos_text: Overall, the hotel was nice, but not very good.
neg_text:
1. I think that for this price (I paid PLN 350 per night - I was at the hotel from 3 p.m. to 7 a.m.) parking and a swimming pool should be included in the price
2. Unfortunately, there was a party above my room until 1 a.m. The staff did not react, although the party often moved to the corridor and there was shouting and slamming of doors 😥 3. Minor shortcomings include a broken shower cabin door and the TV not being turned on via the remote control (tolerable). 😁 4. The worst part of my stay was the morning surprise during settlement. The lady at the reception asked if I had used the bar in the fridge! Since there was something there, I assumed that the hotel staff knew how to take care of the customer and it was a treat. Nothing could be further from the truth. The receptionist added up my bill and charged me over 100 PLN (she added it up on a piece of paper, without a receipt)! I guess you should also charge for toilet paper, tea, soap, etc. If you are going to engage in such "fraud", it would be worth informing the customer that what is in the fridge is paid for! 😥😥😥 Overall impression. Nice hotel, although too focused on profit! I understand that times are hard, but to this extent?

title: Very good
pos_text:
The hotel is generally ok, without any wow factor, but it serves its purpose. I would rate it about 8/10 considering the price.

The hotel itself is not new, you can feel it: The interior and equipment are already slightly outdated, in some places there is a characteristic "age" smell - both in the rooms and at the entrance to the building. Nothing critical, but an update would definitely help.

The main and very significant advantage is the presence of a swimming pool and sauna right in the hotel. This is a really big bonus: You don't need to go anywhere, you can calmly swim and relax in the sauna. It is precisely this convenience that makes the hotel a winner.

The breakfast is quite tasty and good value for money – nothing fancy, but filling and enjoyable.

The location is approximately 30 minutes from the city centre, which is worth considering when planning. Overall, this is a standard hotel, suitable for a short stay or if the on-site pool and sauna are important.

title: Wonderful hotel with a beautiful view from the window and parking!!
pos_text:
Wonderful hotel, very friendly staff, we needed to check in late in the evening and the hotel was always in touch with us.

We stayed with a child under one year old, and although the hotel provides a crib upon request, we did not need it, as the child sleeps with us. I was very pleased with the size of the bed, where the three of us fit perfectly (even taking into account that the child slept across). A pleasant bonus was also the toys that the staff prepared and left in the room in advance so that the child had something to do.

Beautiful views of the old town from the window, a large number of restaurants to suit every taste and budget in the area, and delivery directly to the hotel is also possible; the girls at the reception will pick up your order and notify you.

And of course, the most important thing for traveling by your own transport is that the hotel has parking available, which is a huge plus since the hotel is located in the very center. The price is 50 zlotys per night, which is cheaper than other hotels nearby.

We will definitely choose this hotel again if we are in Wroclaw, thank you very much!

title: Recommendation: Great for an overnight stay, but I suggest eating breakfast elsewhere.
pos_text: Overall Impression: The hotel is in an excellent location—clean and comfortable. The staff is polite.
neg_text:
Breakfast Organization: The management is extremely strange. Despite the price of 120 PLN for two (for comparison, a dinner at the nearby "Konspira" is 150 PLN), the buffet trays were almost empty. This was the case during all three days of our stay, even though we didn't arrive late and the restaurant was nearly empty.

Service in the Restaurant: While the staff politely offered, "We will bring you whatever you like," the subsequent questions about exactly how many eggs or sausages you would eat felt demeaning. Although the food was tasty, the selection was too basic and did not justify the high price.

Conclusion: The hotel itself is wonderful, but the odd management style in the restaurant spoils the overall experience. I found it tedious even to share these details, but I decided to do so since I was sent a feedback survey.

title: Not intended for couples or relaxing weekend trips.
pos_text: The proximity to the train station was advantageous – it takes about a quarter of an hour to get to the old town.
neg_text:
Events were announced via booking (after booking, mind you). I assume it's a bit louder inside the building itself, but not that the bass is thumping into the room until 1 a.m. so much that the bed vibrates. And quite honestly, I'm a good sleeper and very understanding, but... Such noise levels are unacceptable, especially given the hotel's policy of quiet hours from 10 pm to 6 am. After I asked for a discount, I was turned away rudely - according to the motto: I would have received an email and could have cancelled. My hairdryer in the room didn't work, neither did the access card to the room on Saturday, and I had to wait three-quarters of an hour at 10 pm to get into my room (no compensation was offered here either). The receptionists seemed more overwhelmed and annoyed than solution-oriented towards the guests. Keep your eyes open when choosing a job - I say this as a former hotel manager in what was then Berlin Mitte. I must commend the cleaning service. The lady was very helpful and also very friendly.

I cannot comment on breakfast and the restaurant - the bar was closed due to the parties. I hadn't booked breakfast - I wanted to have breakfast later in town.

title: Never again…
pos_text:
There was a tram stop nearby.
The bed was very comfortable and it was relatively clean.
neg_text: Hotel two days before arrival (date is early December) he sent information that they had a special occasion - a company Christmas Eve party. If I were to change the location, hotel prices at that time for such a close date cost at least PLN 500 per night (ignoring the problem of availability on weekends) - I come to Wrocław alone to study. I arrived before 10pm, tired after a full day of education. I think I spent over 20 minutes at the reception, even though I had already provided all the information on booking - invoice details, phone number, and I don't know what else the lady wrote there. I say they already have my number, she says she doesn't have a number. And the hotel called me 2 hours earlier... Typing speed slower than slow. She was supposed to send me the invoice by email right away. The music was blaring beneath me all night long, and the receptionist refused to move my room to a quieter area, even though there was a place on the opposite side of the room. At 4:30 I was woken up by the screams of a couple making love. And I don't think they were very loud at all, but the walls are very thin. A great night as before the next morning classes, waking up at 6:30. The bathroom was recently renovated, but the shower head was probably 20 years old. Hair stuck to the shower walls. But only 4. As if that wasn't enough, the hotel doesn't know how to issue invoices. I waited almost 3 weeks. There was another problem because they were supposedly issuing a correction for a week because someone made a mistake. They responded to messages with a delay of several days. And the atmosphere is a bit like the PRL. All together: a tragedy in its entirety.

Unfortunately, the passage of time is visible in this hotel. During our short stay, we found a few seemingly trivial things that effectively reduced the comfort of our stay:

- leaking shower with missing door seal. Additionally, the door is either damaged or has dropped, which is why the other seals are not closing properly. After every bath, there is just a puddle in front of the shower

- bathroom tiles moving on the floor when you step on them, or a damaged toilet seat, or rather its mounting.

- traces of mold in the shower cabin, the shower faucet is so calcified that it is difficult to set the water temperature

- bathroom fan not working, bathroom door handle loose,

- leaky window, although there are external blinds and it can be somewhat prevented from getting cold air in, but it's weak.

- a worn out bed mattress to the point that when you sleep you just sink in the middle

- no garbage can, except for a mini garbage can in the bathroom. There are tea cups and a kettle - but no spoons or anything to stir the tea with :)

I received a reply via email that the staff would be happy to fulfill my "special request". Upon arrival, I was informed that the hotel does not take parking reservations, and the staff stated that my "special request" was to check in at 2:00 PM (!?). I will just add that there were a dozen or so spaces in the hotel parking lot with the description - "reserved space". This is some kind of misunderstanding. But eventually a parking space was found and that is good news. Unfortunately, this time I have to lower my rating.
neg_text: The room was a real sauna. Air conditioning (according to the information on the plate located next to the control panel) in winter it only works in heating mode and despite being set to the lowest possible 16°C, it only heated the already warm room, and the window in the room cannot be opened or tilted. This is quite a surprise considering the amount you have to pay per night. Unfortunately, this made it difficult to fall asleep and caused constant waking up, which significantly affected the comfort of the stay - I wanted to run away from the room. If I decide to stay again, it will only be in the summer; however, in the winter I will look for another hotel with working air conditioning.

2)The pillows provided were half-empty and incredibly uncomfortable, and there were no others in the room.
The duvets they provide are too heavy even with the air conditioning off and you end up sleeping half uncovered or sleeping in your underwear and feeling cold or in your pajamas and sweating.

3)Cleanliness needs to be reviewed, the lamp above the bathroom mirror had a layer of dust that had accumulated who knows how long ago.

4)More seriously, we stayed for three nights and they failed to clean the room every day, with the result that on the last day we had neither clean tablecloths nor toilet paper rolls.

5) the air conditioner was too powerful and even setting it to 22 degrees (the minimum) meant you were suffocating after a very short time and the lack of an opening window was a problem.

I understand that it is a conference hotel but here we don't even feel the passing grade.

title: I'm in Wrocław two or three times a year - I'll definitely stay there again.
pos_text:
I stayed at this hotel a few months ago. I was disgusted by the breakfast offered. A symbolic choice, stale bread - in one word, a tragedy. This time I was positively surprised: the breakfast was very tasty, the bread was fresh and there was a good selection.
As for the location (as before) I maintain that it is simply wonderful. The hotel's glory days are long gone, but the bedding is clean, the beds are comfortable, and the staff is very nice and helpful. I came back really happy. I can certainly recommend such a stay to anyone. Additionally, the restaurant of the same name honors a 10% discount based on a business card issued by the hotel - and the cuisine is excellent. In short - if Wrocław, this is where it is.

title: EXCELLENT, I highly RECOMMEND it.
pos_text:
The hotel is very well located, in the center of Wrocław, near the Market Square and very well connected to the city's transport system. The hotel facilities, common areas and rooms are in excellent condition. The truth is, the photos don't lie. The beds are comfortable and the shower is spacious, as is the bathroom. The location allows you to enjoy Wroclaw's nightlife without any problems, and during the day you can easily get away to other points of interest or simply explore its museums and historical sites.
The breakfast is EXCELLENT, it has VARIETY and it's FEELING. The food is excellent, and the service from the waiters, as well as the tableware used, is impeccable. The truth is, I was pleasantly surprised.
neg_text: Due to its excellent service, I could not stay longer at the hotel, as I could only stay for ONE (1) DAY, and it does not have its own parking. These are the only TWO (2) observations I can make.

title: Excellent central place
pos_text: The location is especially good - the old town is very close, and you can actually see the Bridge of Penitents from some rooms! Rooms are comfortable and facilities are good. Especially liked the spa, which admittedly was enhanced by us having it to ourselves. As an extra nice touch the hotel has its own Wroclaw Dwarf as well!

title: Very good
pos_text:
The hotel is a short walk from the main square, which is a big advantage. They were very pleasant at the reception. Our room was modern, medium sized. You can refill your water for free in the hallway at any time (incl. soda) from the vending machine.
We visited the wellness center in the evening. We ended up using everything the hotel had to offer, but perfection was lacking in fine-tuning the details.
You can also swim in the pool, but the water was very cold.
The hot tub is in the corner of the pool, ideally for 4 people, so you have to be lucky to get in. It was pleasant and the water was warm.
The sauna is right next to the pool, it is very small, max. for 4 people. Because of its location, people go there in swimsuits.
It's a shame that there are almost no storage spaces or hangers in this one large room. There are only 3 chairs to relax, that's really not enough (but I understand - there's no room there).
The advantage is that there is a water dispenser, towels and possibly spare slippers.
The overall impression of the wellness center depends a lot on how many other people you meet there.
I have to praise the breakfast a lot - a large selection, you can also make fresh juice, good coffee, there is a waffle machine and I recommend trying the cheeses, they are very good.
neg_text:
I would have reservations about the non-functioning safe (but we didn't address it), the bed mattress is very soft and we expected that there would be cream/body lotion available in the bathroom (there was only liquid soap/shower gel). Otherwise, everything you need is here.
The wellness area is smaller and everything is in one place - don't expect privacy or comfort here unless you're very lucky.

title: Wonderful City break to Wrocław
pos_text:
Modern room, thoughtfully furnished. Beautiful bathroom with a walk-in shower; we would have liked more storage space, but the room was very spacious.
The culinary offerings at breakfast are more than just delicious. The fact that high-quality coffee drinks and tea could be prepared in the lobby throughout the day was also very pleasant.
The location of the hotel is excellent. You can quickly reach the old town and get anywhere quickly using public transport.
neg_text: The renovated swimming pool could have more seating; the pool water was very cold at 24 degrees. A ban on mobile phones should also be imposed. A recommendation for parents with children that the swimming pool can be visited until 7:00 pm would also be desirable. We had a very unpleasant encounter with other hotel guests there, but the hotel wasn't really to blame for that.

title: Нужно добавить лежаки или хоть стулья у бассейна, контролировать температуру воды и работу сауны.
pos_text: Прекрасные завтраки как для 4* и местоположение.
neg_text:
Я бронировала этот отель исключительно для того, чтоб иметь бассейн, спортзал и сауну.
В день заезда сначала мне отказали в халате, ссылаясь на то, что отель переполнен и на сегодня уже все халаты разобрали, приходите завтра. потом сказали, что у меня дешёвый номер, так как он на одного человека. ( тут я вообще ничего не поняла, так он и дешевле потому что на одного) или не всем категориям номеров положен халат в отеле с бассейном?
Через час, другой, максимально милый молодой человек, без каких либо вопросов, организовал мне 2 халата в номер за 15 минут.
Но, как оказалось, в тот день бассейн был с очень холодной водой, а сауна практически не грела. Спортзал вообще был закрыт. Лежаков было 3 шт, что очень мало. На второй день бассейн стал чуть теплее, но так же некомфортно, сауна ещё холоднее. Один лежак уже был сломан и их осталось 2. далее я просто заболела, предполагаю, что как раз из-за переохлаждения в их «спа» и третий день уже никуда не ходила, да и смысла не было.
Также на второй день в этом спа уже не было полотенец в раздевалке и постоянно отсутствовал человек на рецепции спа, у которого можно было бы что-то попросить.
Если вам это все не нужно, нормальный средний отель в центре города с хорошими завтраками.

title: Exceptional
pos_text: The hotel is located in the center of the Old Town, within easy walking distance of the train station. Very close to the market square. A very functional room with very good lighting (this area is often lacking in hotels) , with a fridge, in the bathroom a big plus too many hangers and space to put cosmetic bags (often in hotels there is one hanger and the cosmetic bag has to stand on the ground, but here it is not). I know the hotel also has newly renovated rooms, but our room was one of the older ones, but it really had everything we needed. I like that instead of plastic water bottles in the rooms, there is a dispenser with sparkling and still water and a reusable, lockable carafe in the hallway. I like that there are dispensers with washing gel in the bathroom instead of mini cosmetics. A very good idea that room cleaning is at the guest's request. Breakfasts in the restaurant are delicious and varied. The hotel staff were very nice and helpful. We didn't use the swimming pool/fitness area because the hotel was currently renovating it, but we received information that such renovation was taking place at the booking stage.

title: More pros than cons, although nothing groundbreaking.
pos_text: The facility is worth recommending, not only because of the obvious advantage of its location. At Scandic, a certain level is generally guaranteed: the good and varied breakfast was not much worse than what I know from the Scandinavian hotels of this chain, and in terms of comfort, finishing and bathroom equipment it was perhaps even better. The clever layout of the room deserves praise. An additional plus for the helpfulness of the staff - we checked in without any problems, although it was technically over an hour before the scheduled check-in time.
neg_text: The main problem: the promised soundproofing is unfortunately a lie. You can hear quite a lot from the busy Piłsudskiego Street: from accelerating cars to all possible sounds of trams (which have a stop right in front of the window) there were loud conversations and laughter of passers-by. Room decor is a more subjective thing, but I admit that the yellowish wallpaper, which looked like it hadn't been renovated in decades, put me in a rather depressing mood. Sleeping was not made easier by the pillows being too soft: to quote his wife, "to get a good night's sleep, you need pillows that you can rest your head on, not ones you sink into."

title: Good stay overall.
pos_text: The proximity to the old town, the breakfast, the sauna, our bed, the cleanliness, the decor and the staff were very good.
neg_text: The comfort of the extra bed used by our children and the noise it made every time it changed position (it woke us up several times during the night) were below our expectations.

After reading the reviews, we decided to stay at this hotel, but unfortunately, it was a real disappointment. In our humble opinion, this property does not have the characteristics of a 4-star hotel; to be generous, I would only give it 3 stars. The furniture and bathrooms are very dated and the rooms are small. The reception staff was very rude both during check in and also when, the next day, we complained because at 4pm. 00 the rooms had not yet been cleaned. The girl at reception arrogantly said that they should prioritise cleaning the rooms needed for tourists checking in, without even apologising for the inconvenience. So if you want to take a shower or rest for an hour in the afternoon, it's better to change hotels. Cleaning only consisted of changing towels and making the bed in a cursory manner. We found the hair we left on the bathroom floor in the same place. The rooms were equipped with a coffee maker and a kettle. It's a shame that we only found the pods on the first day, from the second day onwards nothing could be done, the machines were only there for show. Breakfast left a lot to be desired. Not much selection in the sweets department. Every day only croissants and a small portion of cake. Only two types of jam and in small quantities, not enough for everyone. Nutella or other non-existent chocolate spread. Every day the same variety of savory dishes and bread, not always fresh that day. In short, a big disappointment, nothing to do with the
other 4-star hotels where we stayed in other cities in Poland.

The gentleman at the reception is also a wonderful, very nice man. Breakfast was not buffet style due to the period (the day before Christmas Eve), but it really exceeded our expectations - we received a package for two people, which, despite good intentions and great hunger, was impossible to finish. Here, too, we were met with the heart of the President and his staff, as our son woke up with a fever and everyone rose to the challenge to improve his well-being. Thank you very much for this short but wonderful, warm, family-like and pleasant stay. When in Wrocław, we will definitely come back here.

title: A stay that somehow managed to be smooth, comfortable, and unexpectedly entertaining.
pos_text:
Every hotel stay blends into the next after a while. Same rooms, same breakfasts, same “it was lovely” reviews that mean absolutely nothing.

This one didn’t.

Not because of anything dramatic. Quite the opposite. It just quietly got everything right. Check-in was smooth, the room was exactly what you want after travelling, and nothing felt like a compromise. No noise, no faff, no “why is this like this?” moments.

But the highlight? Breakfast theatre.

On our second morning, a man arrived in full hotel robe, like he’d taken a wrong turn out of a spa and decided to commit to the look. Fair enough. Except he then proceeded to pace the lobby like a minor celebrity avoiding paparazzi, surveying the room with the kind of calm authority usually reserved for people who own islands.

Nobody stopped him. Nobody questioned it. And somehow, it worked. The whole place carried on as if this was completely normal behaviour.

That moment summed the hotel up better than anything else. Comfortable enough that people relax into it, but polished enough that even a bathrobe power-walk through reception doesn’t break the atmosphere.

Beyond the unexpected entertainment, everything else delivered exactly as it should. Great location for exploring Wrocław, genuinely good breakfast, and staff who manage to be helpful without feeling scripted.

It’s rare to find somewhere that just works without trying too hard.

We’ll be back. And next time, I might bring a robe just to see how far I can push it.
neg_text: The only minor issue was the free candy at reception. A great idea, unless you arrive with children who immediately identify it as a core feature of the hotel. From that point on, leaving or entering the building required a negotiation I was rarely winning.

The breakfasts were delicious, varied and regularly replenished, so everyone could find something for themselves. This stay was one of the nicest and most comfortable hotel experiences we have ever had. - we will definitely come back here again.

title: Exceptional
pos_text:
My adventure with the Radisson Blu Hotel Wrocław began like a good novel – with the first sentence that completely captivated me. The very sight of the hotel, discreetly nestled between the greenery of Słowacki Park and the monumental architecture of the Racławice Panorama, promised history on a grand scale. And he kept his word.


This was no ordinary stay. It was a 5 senses experience.


Sight? I was delighted with the elegance of the interiors – modernity flirting with the classic, light dancing on marble surfaces, a space that breathes peace.

Hearing? I was resting – even though the center of Wrocław was pulsating with life, the silence in the room was so perfect, as if someone had muted the world with a remote control.

Smell? Freshly brewed coffee in the morning led me straight to the Aquarelle restaurant, where breakfast was more of a celebration than a meal.

Taste? Poetry. Every detail of the buffet is refined like a verse in a sonata.

Touch? Perfectly soft bedding that made me briefly consider extending my stay…for a week.


But the real heart of this place is the people. The service was not "professional". She was attentive. Warm. Authentic. The kind that remembers what coffee you drink and asks if the city fulfills your dreams. I felt not like a guest—like the welcome hero of this story.


In the evening, a walk along the Oder River, a few steps from the hotel, had something cinematic about it. I returned to my room, looked at the illuminated Wrocław and thought that there are places worth visiting… and there are some to which we return.


The Radisson Blu in Wrocław is not a hotel.

It's a stage where every guest plays a leading role.

And me? I'm already booking an encore.


⭐ 10/10 — for elegance without pomp, luxury without coldness and memories that stay long after check-out.

pos_text: Platinum Palace Boutique Hotel & SPA is a very elegant and well-maintained hotel, with spacious rooms and a pleasant atmosphere. Unfortunately, the experience was affected by several issues that I did not expect from a hotel of this standard. The staff did not seem adequately trained for a 5-star hotel and, at times, appeared not prepared to manage a luxury property. My room was not cleaned properly during the stay, it was left untidy, and I felt poorly treated when I reported the issue.

I appreciated that the management eventually agreed to a partial refund through Booking.com, but I still believe the compensation was not fully proportionate to the inconvenience experienced. Overall, the hotel has excellent potential thanks to the structure and facilities, but the level of service and guest care needs significant improvement to truly meet 5-star expectations.

Szczególnie zależało mi na wannie – nie mamy jej w domu, więc specjalnie zabrałam ze sobą sole do kąpieli. Niestety przez dwa dni z rzędu woda była jedynie letnia i nieprzyjemna do kąpieli. Wzięcie prysznica również było wyzwaniem z powodu uszkodzonej słuchawki.

W efekcie ani kąpiel, ani prysznic nie były komfortowe, co przy weekendowym wyjeździe było dla mnie dużym rozczarowaniem.

When I pointed out at checkout that a kettle was missing, the friendly receptionist informed me that they could have provided both milk and a kettle upon request.
Unfortunately, there was no prior indication of this, for example in a welcome letter or at check-in. Comfortable second cushions for leaning against, a notepad and pen were also missing; in fact, a welcome folder, perhaps with some tips about the city, was completely absent. These are the kinds of details that should be standard in a hotel that calls itself the most luxurious in Wrocław. Unfortunately, this gives the impression that it was done without care.

neg_text:
When choosing this hotel, I was guided by the option of a swimming pool and jacuzzi. Unfortunately, if someone wants to warm up in the jacuzzi after a long day, there is no such option because the water in the jacuzzi is the temperature of a swimming pool. It is constructed in such a way that water from the pool overflows into the jacuzzi. If I had known this, I don't know if I would have chosen this hotel because it was crucial for me as I am not a fan of saunas.
Another issue is the lighting in the rooms... Unfortunately, if someone wants to get ready to go out after sunset, it is very troublesome because despite turning on all possible lights, the room is still dark... And there are a lot of shadows.
As a mood option it's very nice because this creates a cozy evening mood. However, for me it was definitely too dark... when I wanted to reach for clothes from the closet, I had to use my phone's flashlight to see what I was reaching for, and when it comes to makeup, unfortunately, in the semi-darkness and full of shadows, I couldn't see anything. This is a curiosity for women.

neg_text: I don't have to force myself to look for anything, everything was in order.

title: Pleasant
neg_text:
No windows for ventilation!!
The room smelled unpleasant because the ventilation system wasn't working either. The ventilation in the bathroom also didn't work, so after using the toilet there was an unpleasant smell in the whole room!

Terrible !

title: Stylish, but some staff are arrogant.
pos_text: modern house, high-quality furnishings
neg_text: The room door was very heavy and wouldn't close; I had to complain. The staff were slightly annoyed and arrogant.

title: Exceptional
pos_text: Silence, peace, order. Great staff.

title: Very bad stay
pos_text: The only thing we liked was the location within walking distance of the old town and the sights.
neg_text:
The room was clean but there was a very significant problem: the window did not open and there was no possibility of ventilation, which caused us a very unpleasant feeling during our stay.
Also, the service at the reception was not helpful at all, we didn't understand English well when we asked some of our questions and the employee reacted inappropriately and laughed ironically at each other.
The pillows are quite small, the towels are carpets, you need improvement in both the service and the room amenities.
Breakfast is average with few choices.
They also sent us a message a day before we left asking us to give them a rating of 9 or 10 and if we had any complaints, to report it to the reception and not to leave a negative comment with a low rating.

title: Mid-range hotel near the center
pos_text: Great location, just a few minutes to the city center, and the staff are incredibly friendly...
neg_text: Unfortunately, the windows could not be opened and the shower holder was broken.

title: Superb
pos_text: The service was very nice, the location is a big plus, the room was also very clean
